title: Fix GPS capture bug and add delete asset functionality

requirements.text: Fix the GPS capture bug in the asset entry form (AssetEntryForm.tsx) so that latitude, longitude, and accuracy are correctly captured and attached to new asset submissions. The useGeolocation hook's `capture` callback must be invoked at the right lifecycle point, its state must be read after the async capture resolves, and the resulting coordinates must be included in the submission payload sent to the backend.

quotes: GPS in not working for new asset in this version...pls correct error

acceptanceCriteria: Pressing the GPS capture button in the asset entry form triggers the browser Geolocation API and populates latitude, longitude, and accuracy fields.
The captured GPS coordinates are visible in the form UI before submission.
On form submission, the GPS coordinates are included in the record stored in the backend.
The submitted asset record, when viewed in the admin dashboard detail drawer, displays the correct GPS coordinates.
If geolocation is denied or unavailable, a clear error message is shown to the user and submission is not silently broken.

requirements.text: Add a `deleteAsset` function to the backend Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) that accepts an asset record ID and removes the corresponding entry from the in-memory map. The function must return a success/failure variant indicating whether the record was found and deleted.
acceptanceCriteria: The backend exposes a `deleteAsset(id: Text) : async Result.Result<(), Text>` (or equivalent) function.
Calling deleteAsset with a valid existing ID removes the record and returns a success result.
Calling deleteAsset with a non-existent ID returns an error/not-found result without crashing.
After deletion, querying all assets no longer returns the deleted record.

requirements.text: Add a delete asset capability to the frontend. In the Admin Dashboard (AdminDashboard.tsx), add a 'Delete' action for each asset record — accessible from the asset detail drawer and/or the asset table row. Clicking Delete shows a confirmation dialog before calling the backend deleteAsset function. On success, the asset list is refreshed and a success toast is shown. On failure, an error toast is shown.
acceptanceCriteria: A Delete button is visible for each asset record in the admin dashboard (in the detail drawer or table row, or both).
Clicking Delete shows a confirmation dialog asking the user to confirm before proceeding.
Confirming deletion calls the backend deleteAsset function and removes the record from the displayed list upon success.
A success toast notification is shown after a record is successfully deleted.
An error toast notification is shown if deletion fails.
The useQueries.ts hook includes a `useDeleteAsset` mutation that calls the backend deleteAsset function and invalidates the asset list query on success.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All backend logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
GPS fix must not change the public interface of the useGeolocation hook — only correct its usage in AssetEntryForm.tsx.
nonGoals: Bulk delete of multiple assets at once.
Soft-delete or archive functionality — records should be permanently removed.
Changes to the CSV export format or offline queue logic.
Any changes to the mobile PWA prompt or Add to Home Screen feature.
